name: Bull Terrier
description: The Bull Terrier was developed in 19th-century England from Bulldogs crossed with now-extinct White English Terriers, initially for dog fighting and later refined into a fashionable gentleman's companion. Their egg-shaped head — unique among all dog breeds — and triangular eyes give the Bull Terrier its unmistakable, almost cartoon-like appearance that has made it an advertising icon. Despite a clownish, affectionate personality with people, Bull Terriers can be aggressive toward other animals and require early socialization, consistent training, and owners who appreciate their characteristic stubbornness.

conditions_detail.name: Patellar Luxation
conditions_detail.description: Patellar luxation occurs when the kneecap slips out of its normal groove in the femur. It can be medial (inward) or lateral (outward) and ranges from grade 1 (occasional slipping) to grade 4 (permanent dislocation).
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Grade 1-2 cases are managed conservatively with weight control and physiotherapy. Grade 3-4 typically require surgical correction to deepen the patellar groove and realign the tibial crest.
prevention_tips: Breed from dogs with normal patellar scores
Avoid high-impact activities in affected puppies
